questions.question: Define Test Plan and list any four of its components.
keyPoints: Test Plan is a document outlining scope, approach, resources, and schedule of testing activities
Components: Test Scope, Test Strategy, Test Resources, Test Schedule, Test Deliverables, Entry/Exit Criteria, Risk Management

questions.question: Explain the difference between Test Strategy and Test Plan.
keyPoints: Test Strategy defines high-level testing approach/philosophy
Test Plan is detailed with specific activities, schedules, and resources
Strategy is 'what to achieve', Plan is 'how to achieve'

questions.question: What are Test Entry and Exit criteria? Give two examples of each.
keyPoints: Entry Criteria: conditions before testing starts (e.g., test environment ready, test cases approved)
Exit Criteria: conditions to complete testing (e.g., 95% test coverage, no critical defects)

questions.question: Explain Equivalence Partitioning and Boundary Value Analysis with examples.
keyPoints: Equivalence Partitioning: divide input into valid/invalid groups (e.g., age 1-100 valid, others invalid)
Boundary Value Analysis: test at boundaries (e.g., for 1-100, test 0,1,99,100,101)

questions.question: What is a Traceability Matrix? Explain its importance.
keyPoints: Traceability Matrix maps requirements to test cases
Ensures each requirement has test coverage
Helps identify untested requirements and impacts of changes

questions.question: List any five test deliverables and explain the purpose of test metrics.
keyPoints: Deliverables: Test Plan, Test Cases, Test Scripts, Defect Reports, Test Summary Report, Traceability Matrix
Test Metrics: quantitative measures to assess testing progress (e.g., test execution rate, defect density, test coverage)

questions.question: Explain risk-based testing approach and mention how risks are prioritized.
keyPoints: Risk-based testing prioritizes based on risk assessment
Risks prioritized by likelihood and impact
High-risk areas tested first
Examples: critical functionality, frequently used features

questions.question: Describe the Software Testing Life Cycle (STLC) phases and explain the role of test planning in the overall process.
keyPoints: STLC Phases: Requirement Analysis, Test Planning, Test Case Development, Environment Setup, Test Execution, Test Cycle Closure
Test Planning comes early and defines: scope, strategy, resources, schedule
Planning coordinates all subsequent activities
Determines entry/exit criteria, identifies risks, allocates resources

questions.question: Explain the structure and contents of a comprehensive Test Plan document as per IEEE 829 standard.
keyPoints: Test Plan Identifier, Introduction, Test Items, Feature to be Tested
Test Approach/Strategy, Item Pass/Fail Criteria, Suspension Criteria, Deliverables
Test Environment and Tools, Staff and Training Needs, Schedule, Risks and Contingencies
Approval and Sign-off sections

questions.question: Discuss various black-box testing techniques in detail with suitable examples.
keyPoints: Equivalence Partitioning: divide input into equivalent classes
Boundary Value Analysis: test boundaries
Decision Table Testing: for multiple input combinations
State Transition Testing: for state-based systems
Use Case Testing: based on user scenarios

questions.question: What is test automation? Explain the factors to consider while developing a test automation strategy.
keyPoints: Test Automation: using tools to execute tests automatically
Factors: test stability, frequency of execution, tool selection, skill availability
ROI calculation, maintenance effort, framework design
Common tools: Selenium, QTP, LoadRunner
